NSE:GREAVESCOT.name: Greaves Cotton Ltd
NSE:GREAVESCOT.businessSummary: Diversified engineering company — automotive components, power products, and EV mobility.

notes.announcementTitle: Incorporation of WOS in Dubai UAE
notes.insight: Greaves Cotton has incorporated a wholly owned subsidiary Greaves International Trading FZE in Dubai UAE (effective 18 Jun 2026). This signals intent to build an international distribution / export channel, likely for EV components or power products. Worth monitoring for revenue contribution in 6–12 months.

notes.announcementTitle: Incorporation of WOS in Dubai UAE (duplicate filing)
notes.insight: Duplicate filing for the same Greaves International Trading FZE (Dubai) WOS incorporation announced today. Same event as bv1swc09abyci8pv46bbc140.pdf — no new information. Greaves Cotton is building international trading infrastructure in UAE, consistent with their EV and power products export ambitions.

NSE:BLISSGVS.name: Bliss GVS Pharma Ltd
NSE:BLISSGVS.businessSummary: Bliss GVS Pharma is a specialty pharmaceutical manufacturer focused on suppositories, soft gelatin capsules, and anti-malarial drugs. It has a significant Africa/emerging markets export business.

notes.announcementTitle: Record Date Fixed for Final Dividend FY2025-26
notes.insight: Bliss GVS Pharma has fixed a record date for payment of final dividend for FY ended March 31, 2026, with the 41st AGM scheduled July 15, 2026. The dividend quantum was not disclosed in this filing. This signals the company is distributing profits for FY26 and the AGM will ratify the dividend. For shareholders, ensure shareholding is recorded before the record date to be eligible. Not a material fundamental development — monitor the AGM outcome and FY26 annual results for more substance.

NSE:SHRIPISTON.name: Shriram Pistons & Rings Ltd
NSE:SHRIPISTON.businessSummary: SPR Auto Technologies (formerly Shriram Pistons & Rings) manufactures pistons, rings, and engine components for automotive OEMs. A key Tier-1 auto-ancillary supplier in India.

notes.announcementTitle: Communication to Shareholders Regarding TDS/Withholding Tax on Dividend
notes.insight: The board has recommended a final dividend of Rs. 5/- per share (face value Rs. 10/-) for FY2025-26, subject to shareholder approval at the 62nd AGM on July 27, 2026; record date is July 20, 2026. This routine TDS communication confirms a dividend will be paid by August 25, 2026 — positive for income investors. It also notes the company has been renamed SPR Auto Technologies Limited, which may signal a broader brand repositioning. Watch for the AGM outcome and any management commentary on the name change rationale.

NSE:SAREGAMA.name: Saregama India Ltd
NSE:SAREGAMA.businessSummary: Saregama India is the largest music label in India, owning one of the world's largest catalogues of Indian music. Revenue streams include music licensing, the Carvaan device, and OTT/digital rights.

notes.announcementTitle: Disclosure under SEBI Takeover Regulations — Composure Services Pvt Ltd
notes.insight: Composure Services Pvt Ltd filed a Regulation 31(4) disclosure under the SEBI Takeover Regulations, indicating that an entity has crossed or is close to crossing a significant shareholding threshold (typically 5%, 10%, or a control threshold). This type of SAST disclosure is a signal that a strategic or financial investor is building or adjusting a stake in Saregama. If it marks a meaningful stake increase, it could indicate growing investor interest in Saregama's content IP and digital licensing business. The counterparty identity (Composure Services) is not widely known — monitor for further stake disclosures or open market purchases in subsequent filings.

NSE:AXISCADES.name: AXISCADES Technologies Ltd
NSE:AXISCADES.businessSummary: AXISCADES is an engineering R&D services company focused on aerospace, defense, automotive, energy, and industrial domains. It is undergoing a strategic transformation involving divestment of its heavy engineering/automotive/aerospace engineering services vertical.

notes.announcementTitle: Credit Rating placed on Rating Watch with Developing Implications
notes.insight: CARE Ratings has placed AXISCADES' long-term bank facilities (Rs. 21 Cr) and combined long/short-term facilities (Rs. 80 Cr) on Rating Watch with Developing Implications (RWD) — directly linked to the company's announced divestment of its Engineering Services business (heavy engineering, automotive, energy, aerospace) disclosed on May 26 and June 12, 2026. A RWD rating means the outcome — upgrade or downgrade — depends on how the divestment is executed and what the resulting company looks like. If the divested business is the core revenue engine, the remaining entity's credit profile could weaken significantly. Conversely, if proceeds are used to deleverage, it could be credit-positive. This is a transitional moment: watch for details on the divestment deal structure (valuation, buyer, retained business) and how management plans to redeploy the capital.

NSE:CONFIPET.name: Confidence Petroleum India Ltd
NSE:CONFIPET.businessSummary: Confidence Petroleum is one of India's largest LPG cylinder manufacturers and distributors, also engaged in automotive LPG/CNG retail and industrial gas solutions.

notes.insight: Confidence Petroleum filed an investor presentation with the exchanges on June 23, 2026. The PDF content was not extractable, limiting insight. Such presentations typically update investors on operational performance, capacity utilization, and near-term guidance. Watch for the uploaded version on the company website (www.confidencegroup.co) for specific KPIs — particularly LPG cylinder volumes, CNG station additions, and EBITDA margin trajectory.

NSE:RPTECH.name: Rashi Peripherals Ltd
NSE:RPTECH.businessSummary: Rashi Peripherals (RP Tech) is one of India's largest IT product distributors, representing global technology brands across compute, peripherals, networking, and components. It is pivoting from pure hardware distribution toward higher-margin enterprise and solutions.

notes.announcementTitle: Board Approves Acquisition of 67% stake in VDA Infosolutions Pvt Ltd for Rs. 368.5 Cr
notes.insight: RP Tech has approved acquisition of a 67% controlling stake in VDA Infosolutions Pvt Ltd (a Mumbai-based enterprise IT solutions and services company) for Rs. 368.5 crores cash consideration — implying an EV of ~Rs. 550 crores (~0.65x FY26 revenue of Rs. 850 Cr). VDA had net worth of Rs. 234 Cr and total assets of Rs. 427 Cr as of March 2026. This is a transformative move: RP Tech is buying a services/solutions company to accelerate its pivot from low-margin product distribution (~1-2% EBITDA) to higher-margin managed services and enterprise IT. The deal valuation looks reasonable at ~0.65x revenue for a profitable IT services firm. Key risks: integration complexity (distribution vs. services DNA), funded by cash (monitors balance sheet leverage) and execution risk in scaling VDA under RP Tech's umbrella. Watch for deal completion timeline, financing structure, and whether VDA's existing enterprise clients cross-sell RP Tech's distribution portfolio.

NSE:GODREJIND.name: Godrej Industries Ltd
NSE:GODREJIND.businessSummary: Godrej Industries is the holding company of the Godrej Group, with interests in chemicals (surfactants, oleochemicals), real estate (Godrej Properties), financial services (Godrej Capital), agri (Godrej Agrovet), and consumer goods (Godrej Consumer Products).

notes.announcementTitle: Outcome of Postal Ballot — Resolution Passed with Requisite Majority
notes.insight: Godrej Industries' postal ballot dated May 15, 2026 has concluded with the resolution passed with requisite majority (voting closed June 22, 2026). The specific resolution detail was not captured in the filing summary — the full postal ballot notice from May 15 would reveal what shareholders approved (typically could be a related party transaction, fundraise, or governance change for a holding company like GIL). This confirms the board can proceed with whatever corporate action was proposed last month. Watch for the follow-through implementation announcement to understand the materiality.

NSE:HARDWYN.name: Hardwyn India Ltd
NSE:HARDWYN.businessSummary: Hardwyn India manufactures architectural hardware products including door fittings, glass fittings, and access control hardware. Listed on NSE SME platform.

notes.announcementTitle: Corrigendum to First EGM Notice — July 03, 2026
notes.insight: Hardwyn India has issued a corrigendum to its First Extraordinary General Meeting notice, scheduled for July 3, 2026 via VC/OAVM. Corrigenda to EGM notices typically correct an error in the original notice — often around resolution wording, eligibility dates, or voting details. The substance of what shareholders will vote on at the EGM remains unknown without reading the original notice. Watch for the EGM outcome on July 3 to understand what corporate action (fundraise, structural change, or special resolution) was being sought.

NSE:AEROENTER.name: Aeroflex Enterprises Ltd
NSE:AEROENTER.businessSummary: Aeroflex Enterprises manufactures stainless steel flexible hoses and assemblies for industrial, defense, and infrastructure applications.

notes.announcementTitle: NSE Sought Clarification on Significant Price Movement
notes.insight: NSE has formally sought clarification from Aeroflex Enterprises regarding significant movement in its stock price on June 23, 2026 — this is a surveillance action triggered when a stock moves sharply without a disclosed corporate reason. The company's reply is awaited. Such queries are typically followed by either a company confirmation that there is no undisclosed material information, or disclosure of a pending corporate event (order win, acquisition, fundraise, etc.). If the price spike was driven by speculation and no material event exists, expect a price correction. If a corporate event is disclosed in the reply, this could be a high-significance catalyst. Watch closely for the company's response filing within 24 hours.

NSE:APOLLO.name: Apollo Micro Systems Ltd
NSE:APOLLO.businessSummary: Apollo Micro Systems designs and manufactures defense and aerospace electronic systems — embedded electronics, avionics, and electronic warfare solutions primarily for Indian defense PSUs and DRDO.

notes.announcementTitle: Allotment of 21.43L Equity Shares on Conversion of Warrants at Rs. 114/share
notes.insight: Apollo Micro has allotted 21,43,095 equity shares at Rs. 114/share to three allottees (Reena Karwa, Superstar Investments, Trikaya Wealth Advisors) upon conversion of warrants originally issued June 2025. Total proceeds received: Rs. 18.32 crores. The conversion was at Rs. 85.50 warrant exercise price (25% paid upfront; remainder now). Paid-up capital increases to 37.16 crore shares — incremental dilution of ~0.58%, which is modest. This is a part-conversion of a larger batch of 3.8 crore warrants issued in 2025; remaining ~3.59 crore warrants are still outstanding and will result in further dilution over time. With the stock likely trading above Rs. 114, conversion is economically rational for warrant holders. Monitor the pace of remaining warrant conversions as a potential supply overhang on the stock.

NSE:SETL.name: Standard Engineering Technology Ltd
NSE:SETL.businessSummary: Standard Engineering Technology Ltd is a listed SME company. Business details are limited — likely involved in engineering or technology manufacturing.

notes.announcementTitle: NSE Sought Clarification on Significant Price Movement
notes.insight: NSE has sought a clarification from Standard Engineering Technology Ltd on June 23, 2026 regarding significant movement in its stock price. This is a standard surveillance query for unusual price/volume activity. The company's reply is pending. For small-cap/SME companies like SETL, such queries can sometimes precede announcement of contract wins, promoter stake sales, or fundraising events. However, they can also reflect speculative activity with no underlying corporate development. Watch for the company's response and whether it is followed by a material disclosure.

NSE:MARINE.name: Marine Electricals (India) Ltd
NSE:MARINE.businessSummary: Marine Electricals manufactures power distribution systems, switchgear, and electrical panels — primarily for marine vessels, data centers, and industrial applications.

notes.announcementTitle: Order win of Rs. 75.19 Cr from STT Global Data Centres and Princeton Digital Group
notes.insight: Marine Electricals has won two orders totalling Rs. 75.19 crores (ex-tax) from STT Global Data Centres India and Princeton Digital Group India — both marquee data center operators — for supply of power distribution systems over 12-18 months. This is a meaningful order in the context of India's booming data center capex cycle, and confirms Marine Electricals is establishing itself as a credible power distribution vendor for hyperscale/co-location DCs. Two separate blue-chip data center clients in a single announcement suggests a broadening customer base beyond traditional marine applications. Watch for whether data center revenue becomes a recurring order stream and how this compares to total order book / annual revenue to gauge materiality.

NSE:GPIL.name: Godawari Power & Ispat Ltd
NSE:GPIL.businessSummary: Godawari Power & Ispat is an integrated steel manufacturer based in Chhattisgarh, producing iron pellets, sponge iron, billets, and wire rods. It also has captive power generation.

notes.announcementTitle: 6.91 MW Waste Heat Recovery Power Plant Commences Commercial Operations
notes.insight: GPIL's 6.91 MW Waste Heat Recovery Based (WHRB) power plant at its Siltara, Raipur facility has commenced commercial operations on June 23, 2026 — announced earlier on April 2, 2026. The plant utilises waste heat from the Pellet Plant and Ferro Alloys Division flue gas, converting it into captive power. This reduces GPIL's grid power dependency and lowers per-unit energy costs, which is directly margin-accretive for a steel manufacturer where power is a major input cost. While 6.91 MW is relatively modest in the context of an integrated steel plant, it represents a step toward operational self-sufficiency. Watch for total captive power capacity post this addition and management commentary on power cost savings per tonne in the next quarterly result.

NSE:HONASA.name: Honasa Consumer Ltd
NSE:HONASA.businessSummary: Honasa Consumer (Mamaearth parent) is a digital-first House of Brands in beauty and personal care — operating Mamaearth, Derma Co, Aqualogica, Dr. Sheth's, Staze, and BBlunt. It sells primarily via D2C and modern trade with a digital-first go-to-market.

notes.announcementTitle: Acquisition of 58% stake in Fluence Pharma Pvt Ltd at ~Rs. 135 Cr EV; WOS Honasa Health incorporated
notes.insight: Honasa has acquired a controlling 58% stake in Fluence Pharma Pvt Ltd — a nutraceuticals company with patented Cyclical Nutrition Therapy (CNT), dermatologist distribution in 3,000+ clinics, and brands Hair Fact/Skin Fact/Pro Fact — at ~Rs. 135 crores EV (implying ~3.4x FY26 revenue of Rs. 40 Cr and ~17x EV/EBITDA at 20% margins). The remaining 42% will be acquired in two tranches over 5-7 years. Honasa will also incorporate 'Honasa Health Private Limited' as a WOS to run B2C nutraceuticals operations. This marks a deliberate strategic pivot: Honasa is moving beyond topical beauty into 'inside-out' supplements, tapping India's Rs. 16,000 Cr+ vitamins and supplements market growing at 11% CAGR. The acquisition is competitively logical — Derma Co already has a clinical-dermatologist channel, and Fluence's practitioner trust (3,000+ dermatologists) directly complements it. Key risks: nutraceuticals is a new category with different regulatory and formulation complexity; valuation multiples appear full for a Rs. 40 Cr revenue company; management bandwidth stretched across 6+ brands. Watch for contribution of nutraceuticals revenue in Q2/Q3 FY27 and whether the Fluence science can scale via D2C channels.

NSE:KIRLOSENG.name: Kirloskar Oil Engines Ltd
NSE:KIRLOSENG.businessSummary: Kirloskar Oil Engines (KOEL) manufactures diesel and gas engines, gensets, and pump systems for agriculture, industrial, and power generation applications. It is a leading engine maker with export presence.

notes.announcementTitle: Multiple Investor Call Intimations — June 26 and June 29, 2026
notes.insight: KOEL has scheduled 5 separate investor calls over June 26 (at 2:45 PM, 3:30 PM, 4:15 PM) and June 29 (at 10:30 AM, 11:15 AM) — an unusually high density of investor meets in a short period. This pattern is consistent with a management roadshow, often timed around quarterly results or a significant corporate development that management wants to proactively communicate to institutional investors. Watch for whether a Q1 FY27 result, analyst day, or corporate action announcement follows these calls, which could explain the urgency.

NSE:SIS.name: SIS Ltd
NSE:SIS.businessSummary: SIS Ltd is one of India's largest security services and facility management companies, operating across guarding, cash logistics (SIS Cash Services), and electronics security. It has significant operations in Australia via SIS Australia.

notes.announcementTitle: Board to Consider Buyback of Equity Shares on June 29, 2026
notes.insight: SIS Ltd's board has scheduled a meeting on June 29, 2026 to consider and approve a buyback of equity shares. A buyback signals management's confidence in the company's intrinsic value exceeding the current market price and is shareholder-friendly — it reduces share count, boosts EPS, and returns cash more tax-efficiently than dividends. For SIS, this comes at a time when the security services sector is experiencing steady demand growth from corporates and government contracts. The buyback size, method (open market vs. tender offer), and pricing will be critical — watch for the board outcome on June 29 to assess materiality. If the buyback price is at a meaningful premium to CMP, expect a sharp positive price reaction.
